reviews.review_text: Very friendly and helpful staff
An absolute must stay on your way to or from Exmouth or traveling by. Great vibe, heaps to do and see a great experience.

Amazing Location. The land is beautiful.
Sheep cows and horses roam.
A couple of well maintained and easy grade walks.
Don't miss Sunset Hill.
Very popular for campers with caravans, and families, and singles and duos rocking the rooftop tents.

Great stop over on the way to or from Exmouth.
Huge accommodation area, coffee, food and bar available.

We are 3 ladies in a Maui hired van and camped for 3 days at Bullara station. Lovely happy place.  Staff welcoming and friendly. Great place for kids and adults. Loved the homemade scones and pies from the cafe. We also booked a dinner and enjoyed meeting other travelers from around the world. Definitely will be back one day.
Great country-style campsite, well organised with a nice café and walk trails.

Whilst we were not staying at the station overnight,  you can still visit the station for a day trip from Exmouth.  When we arrived it was around 12pm and most of the pies were sold,  so I recommend getting in early if you want more choice. The scones were delicious too. The restaurant is only open to guests staying the night and the bar wasn't open until 4pm, again mostly for guests. The surrounds were so impressive and we got to see some of the quirky characteristics of the property such as the fun showers and the art.

Even if you are not staying,  the station is still a great attraction to pop in for a quick explore.

I hadn’t been back to this place for about 18 years. Doing a lap of Oz (Lap4luvo) and thought I’d drop in for the night, camping in a tent. Well!! It didn’t disappoint. I lobbed on a Friday night which was burger night. The owners and staff of Bullara have done an amazing job. Blow Coral Bay off and make this your go-to!!!
TOP SPOT!

I found this place very pricey happy half hour was only selected drinks.Went for dinner at $78 a head one pork spare rib per person. What I call potato bake and a lettice salad and a little serve of cracking a bun for starters with cheese cake for dessert.  My husband actually walked out hungry making sure he didn't eat all day to make sure he could eat everythink since it was a 3 cause meal. My husband isn't a big person and he doenst have a huge appetite.  I do understand they have been through a flood but the prices are extreme.

Our second stay here - for longer this time. We were in a caravan on a powered site - overlooking bush.
We just love it here - planning our next trip already. Staff are great, the vibe at the Woolshed is the best, and the food…the beef, the scones…to die for. Glad we got to experience burger night and a dinner at the Table this time - out of this world…
What a great outback stay. Staff and facilities are perfect.

An absolutely awesome place to stay, the amazing staff do everything possible to ensure that you have a great experience. This includes leading you to your camp-site so you can make sure you're on the right site. Highly recommend the dinner at the "Long Table", it was delicious! To think this station was recently hit by a cyclone and was 2 metres under water, the staff clearly worked very hard to get it operational again.

INCROYABLE. Le meilleur camping de notre roadtrip. L'accueil est génial, les gens adorables. Les douches sont propres et chaudes. Les toilettes sont propres. Il y a internet. La cuisine est bien et en plus le bar avec la musique live c’était génial ! On est revenu pour une 2ème nuit car on se sentait bien là bas ! Vraiment au top je recommande.

Absolutely amazing place to visit and stay, everyone is extremely welcoming!!
The amenities, cafe etc is stunning and fantastic for the family to look at and enjoy.
100% going to be returning when we can make it up this way 😍😎
Shout out to Emily for being attentive and noticing it was my birthday! It’s the small things that matter at Bullara.
A great place to stop off with the van.  Highly recommend Burger night.
WOW!! Unreal experience - especially ‘Burger Night’ on a Friday night. Live music, delicious food and brilliant atmosphere….11/10!!
Fantastic place to stay on our half lap. We still tall fondly of burger night! We will be back some day
HOMEAWAY movie shoot incredible place
Amazingly lovely people, well worth a visit ❤️

Great place to visit, one of the top two best pies I've ever eaten!! People are very friendly and the Scones were amazing. We'll be back to stay in 2026 for sure! Thanks Bullara, Jarret - Gecko Adventures Australia
🌎✝️⛪🫂The Real Negara Besar🌄🛏️✌🏻😁

Wow. We’ve  spent 5 night tent camping at Bullara Station. It was our first family camping trip, and after 13 nights on the Ningaloo Coast I was worried that the stay at Bullara would be too long and slightly uncomfortable due to being surrounded by heat and dust, and not by an ocean breeze. But really, I loved it so much. Aside from having fantastic amenities (camp kitchen, bathrooms, a cafe, restaurant and bar), it’s really place with a soul. Staff are helpful and happy. Almost every space and surface is an opportunity for artistic expression, a loving reclaiming of old materials, lots of wood and rust. There’s also a great vibe. Our first night was on their famous weekly “burgers and live-music” night. I can only assume that the turnout of around 500 people, most staying for the whole evening, means we’re not the only ones who enjoyed ourselves. We also loved their cafe with strong coffee, fresh scones and meat pies with delicately delicious pastry, and their evening restaurant which showcases their beef and locally sourced produce. And Bullara seems to just be a friendly place. Even the cows are friendly. Tips for staying there? Decide whether you want to be near the camp kitchen or not when requesting a site. Since we used the kitchen a lot we were thankful to be nearby. Go on the walks at sunrise or sunset and take in splendour of WA’s red earth. Treat yourself to the scones, for sure! If camping isn’t for you, stay in one of their lodges our houses or glamping tents. But definitely go!

What a great place...best Coffee in WA!! Worth the drive just for a visit to the Cafe!
Wonderful people working here...with a "nothings too hard" attitude. We'll be back in 2026!!
April 2026 ..they open again!

Where do I start. The setting is rustic and beautiful. The lava showers add a fun touch (seriously you haven't been to Bullara if you don't try the lava showers). Burger night was fun and the burgers were delish. The scones and coffee were to die for. |We bought some snags and burgers before leavIng too so had a few nights of delish meals after leaving. |We had a truly wonderful time and would recommend stopping in.
Great place but a bit over rated, needs play ground equipment for the kids and would love to be able to do a tag along tour of the station to see how they work etc
Cool shower, but in Oct. you'll be hot again in no time. No sign of any campers during the day, must have AC in your camper. Couldn't wait until sundown for dinner, had to take off.

Access was easy. Staff were delightful from booking to exiting. Campsite roomy. The scones!!!…..I’m not a scone lover, but had to buy the apron with the recipe to recreate. Soooo good. |Burger Night deserves its own review. Organising the feeding of 480 people the night we were there was incredible, like a military manoeuvre. The entertainment on the night was brilliant & so connected to the audience. The burger itself, fantastic. The vibe of the night, the best. You’ll have terrible regret if you stay here & don’t do burger night. |We stayed there last two nights of the season. That in itself was pretty special. |Left a message in a bottle for friends from back home who recommended this as a must see on our Big Lap, they’re returning 2026. Such a cool idea. |Brought back childhood memories growing up on a Droughtmaster Stud💕
As usual, another good stay and excellent burger night with entertainment. Well done Edwina, Tim & Team Bullara Staff, we will be back again next year for the season's last burger night.
Pretty good place to stop.  Well organized, interesting, night time food was limited to the long table which was not for us.

Bullara Station was honestly one of our absolute favourites during our 5-week trip along the west coast!  We’d already read great things about Bullara, so we booked well in advance — and it totally lived up to the hype! The place has that real Aussie outback vibe, with a super cozy bar and campfire, lovely staff, and the best morning double espresso.
We ended up staying four nights because we just didn’t want to leave. The hamburgers and minced beef from the freezer at reception were delicious, and the pies… just wow, 5 stars! We even bought a shirt from their merchandise — that’s how much we loved it. Can’t wait to come back someday!

Delightful!||We stayed in a safari tent for 2 nights and enjoyed the experience, we had 2 teenagers which was a challenge as the sleeping was in a communal space but I loved that we had to bunk in and be close like when they were little kids!||The tent was charming and the deck and view priceless. My only gripe is that both zips on the front ‘door’ was broken and so you could not just have the fly screen open for breeze and views and the canvas zip didn’t reach the ground which is an issue given you really are in nature and there are lots of creepy crawlies … at $500 a night this needs fixing as a priority. ||I asked for an early check in as we were staying close by and wanted to drop our stuff off and it was a firm no.||Cade was charming and coffee was great. We had pizza from the visiting wood fired van and it was delish but the wait was 1hr 45 so order early. A drink at the bar with live music was magic 👌
Bullara station is a fantastic place to stay well done to the owners and staff to make it a great place to stay with be back
Casual friendly vibe, great staff and wonderful station setting. Rustic ablutions and artwork are very fun and interesting to look at.

Every now and then, the road throws you a bone. A place you didn’t plan for, didn’t expect, and suddenly it rewrites the trip. That’s this spot a working cattle station that figured out how to welcome the wandering caravan crowd without losing its soul.

There’s a lot going on here, a lot to unpack. For starters, the café. Not some dusty outpost slinging instant coffee in chipped mugs. No. This was the best cup I’ve had between Exmouth and Coral Bay - rich, sharp, the kind of brew that makes you sit up a little straighter. Add fresh-baked scones, jam, cream so thick it defies gravity, and something in your brain just snaps: how the hell is this happening here?

We came back for dinner, and thank God we booked. One dish, that’s it. No laminated menu with a thousand options nobody asked for. Just beef from their own herd, paired with whatever else they could pull in from around the region. That night it was chilli beef - deep, warming, unapologetic. On Fridays? Word is they go primal with a burger night, meat on the grill, smoke in the air, no pretension.

This place doesn’t just feed you, it reminds you why you’re out here in the first place. To stumble into the unexpected. To taste something honest. It’s now in our top ten spots in Australia, and not because it’s polished or perfect—but because it’s real.
Scones are really great! And the place is so nice and relaxing. Loved this stop 🐮

Booked at short notice and staff bent over backwards to accommodate us and found a site we could have for 2 nights which we were very grateful for. The station certainly lives up to its 5 star reputation so impressed I bought a merch hot pink shirt and proudly wear it. A wonderful relaxing experience. Absolutely recommend you don’t overlook this station stay and if you have children they look like they’re having the time of their life. We are grey nomads and really enjoyed the family friendly atmosphere.

Had a great time! We loved staying here on the way up to Exmouth. Everyone was so lovely and welcoming as we arrived. Definitely worth the stop if you're heading up this way, especially with their pizza and live music nights on Thursday's!!

A family holiday with my sister visiting from NZ ….. and our children and grandchildren camping there ….grand final day was excellent with great facilities to watch the game and the staff were amazing … so friendly and helpful … and very family oriented….we particularly loved the long table dinner and the shearer’s quarters served us very well ….. unfortunately it was too hot for us to enjoy the walks so we’ll be back. To top it off the massage lady was an added bonus and we both thoroughly enjoyed it

I absolutely loved my stay at Bullara Station! This place has such an authentic outback feel. Working in Hospitality myself I am probably the worst guest to have and see everything. But Bullara Station nailed it! From the warm hospitality to the rustic charm that makes you feel instantly at home. It’s the perfect mix of comfort and character, giving you a real taste of station life in WA. The atmosphere is relaxed yet vibrant, and you can tell every detail has been thought through to create a genuine experience. I woke up super early and almost bumped into a goat wandering about; it doesn’t get more authentic than that! Bullara Station is a true gem in the outback. Can’t recommend it highly enough!

Wrote earlier review with regard to not getting a refund after my wife who just got out of hospital couldn’t stay here because of no powered sites. Understood the t + c’s for late cancelation for site and was willing to wear financial loss for sake of my wife’s well being. However I was miffed by no refund on meal I had ordered unless they on sell the ticket. I just rang my friend to email them to use instead of us, and was told they wouldn’t honour those ticket, and that they couldn’t do that. When they asked if they could buy a ticket said that they couldn’t. Somethings rotten in Bullara/ Denmark it seems. Sound like to me they sold our tickets but are keeping quiet on refunding us the moneys. Looks it’s only $40 bucks but obviously that means more to them than me. As I said before Greed is the Creed here. Just letting you guys know where the scruples of this mob lie.

Beautiful and authentic outback experience!! The Wild West/ rustic barn aesthetic is incredible, with amazing quirky touches and SO MUCH attention to detail whether it’s the accommodation, restaurant/cafe or even just the toilets and showers. Such a warm and welcoming atmosphere, the staff are so genuine with their hospitality and attentiveness (shout out to Dave who was an amazing camp host)! The food is also super high quality with so much effort put into sustainability and locally sourcing fresh ingredients (evening meals book out a month in advance so be prepared). Couldn’t recommend this place enough!! It’s a MUST for West Coast road trips 🏜️🐂 🌾🌞
We stopped here on our way to Exmouth. What a cool place. Highly recommend a visit here. The scones and pies are the bomb!

Eine wunderschöner Aufenthalt
Etwas Besonders
Stopped in for scones and coffee and it didn't disappoint ! Outstanding coffee and chai and the scones were fluffy with the perfect amount of cream and jam.
Very clean night life is great staff are welcoming

Absolutely fantastic place.
The burger nights are the best.
The staff are so friendly from the check in until the time you leave.(you can tell they love their jobs).
Nothing is too much trouble.
This was our first stay and it will definitely not be our last.
We where told how good this place is and it was so much better.

See you next time.👍👍👍
Brian and Tonia.
Friendly easy going staff, they must be well looked after by their boss. Well done 👍🏼

Absolutely the highlight of our trip!|Bullara Station blew us away, from the moment we arrived, it had the perfect mix of rustic charm and incredible hospitality. ||The dinner was exquisite, so good we went back the second night! (We would’ve gone the third too, but it was pizza night — still fun!) The bike and walking tracks were awesome for exploring, dirt box track for kids and the lava showers very cool! ||Plus don’t even get me started on the scones… divine. And the toasties? Absolute game changers — get one in your belly, seriously. |These guys know their meats, foods  and cooking their pastries. Thank you 🤌||I didn’t make a single coffee for 3 days because the on-site coffee shop was that good. ||A Big shoutout to the whole team — your warmth, energy, and passion for the place shines through. We’ll be recommending Bullara to every traveller heading your way.||Best stop so far — hands down. 👏👏👏

Overrated and Overpriced.||We are currently staying at Bullara Station as we had heard it's a fantastic place and was rated right up there!!|We've been on the road for 5 months and it's probably the least of our trip highlight so far..|Yes, it does have a quirky little shower block which is bloody awesome but I'm struggling to think of any more positives.|In my opinion, it's for cashed up tourists who don't mind spending a buck.|On arrival, we were told there were places left at the table for their cooked dinner... $76pp...! No thanks...WOW, We've just come from Pardoo where a flown in chef did a seafood night for $35pp..Best tucker we have had in the trip thus far...|Firepits are communal, and wait,  there's more, you can't collect any wood from their vast station, but you can purchase a bag for $36....|Neighbours went over to the bar, 2 drinks $25...|We're camped in the scrub out the back.|It did have a water tap which was great.|When booking, pictures showed a pool.|When enquiring about the pool, they said it was a private pool in a private residence but they couldn't remove the picture from Google Images...|I'm giving it a 3* average rating. In my opinion, not great but not terrible.|Very overpriced, glad we experienced it, but will drive past next time.
What a wonderful stay, the local meat was the best l have ever tasted. Watering great to drink. Lots of flies but that's to be expected. Highly recommend

Could be a cool place to stay, but poorly ran.

It appears there used to be multiple access roads to the property, but only one was open when we were there. Also this access road is not in the best shape, lots of holes and washboarding.

To check in we were told we had to be there before 530 so that someone could ride there bike along with us, to show us our spot. There are still staff around working after that, just not the bicycle guy and I guess they don’t trust guests to read the map and go to the clearly marked spots you get assigned.

Also we were able to get a couple nights in a powered site, but had to switch to unpowered due to availability for our other nights. If you do this be aware that their policy is that you check out of your current spot by 930, but then wait around until 10 for the bicycle guy to be ready to show you to your new spot. Which you can just check out early and head to town, but for some reason you still have to be back by 530 before the bicycle guy is done for the day.  We had a full day and they refused to tell us our new site number and that we had to be back before 530. AGAIN, guests can’t be trusted to move to the other spot on your own even though you’ve already been staying there and understand the layout/rules/etc.

There is a cafe. It’s weirdly organized so that if you order a drink you have to wait for it outside the cafe for it to get placed in the window, but if you order food you have to wait inside for the same person to hand it to over the counter. Which isn’t terrible except when you order food AND a drink like I did. When the drink was ready first they made me walk outside instead of handing it to me. Then when the food was ready they made me come back inside instead of handing it to me at the window.  It felt like I was being pranked.

Be aware this cafe closes in the middle of the day with the only food option for dinner being a pricey preset meal. It is reservation required and you apparently have to book before you arrive because when we checked in we were told it was sold out for every night we were there.

Bullara station is “conveniently” located between Exmouth and coral bay, but still a bit of a drive to both. You could go to town for dinner, but then you risk the long ride back in the dark with wildlife on the roads. Which leaves cooking your own food. I recommend being self sufficient and capable of cooking with your camper. There is a camp kitchen, but it’s not is good shape. Used it one night but only has two grills and a gas stove/oven. One of the grills was out of gas and people were waiting in line to use the other. Was able to get the oven lit after the person ahead of us gave up. However it couldn’t get up to temperature and ended up just having to wait in line for the grill.

There are some neat rustic bathrooms and showers along with a couple nicer ones, but not very many of them. Wasn’t uncommon to wait for one.

The attitude here is a battle between an upscale fancy ranch themed campground and an actual ranch that is putting forth the minimum effort for guests to stay. Sadly it seems this place has expanded to fit more guests, but they didn’t really grow to truly accommodate everyone. If you’re far from town and require people to be there at a certain time for check-ins or moving spots you should be able to guarantee people can have food, be it their fancy table dinner, a simple item or two that could be ordered, or a decent kitchen for people to make their own.

It does have a decent atmosphere, but they need to relax a little on the rules, policies, and procedures. Also put some of the money from guests back into making sure that the road is decent for campers to travel on and the amenities can accommodate everyone. I believe this to be the bare minimum for the price we paid. Maybe I’m being harsh, but I was excited to stay here only for it to be frustrating and annoying anytime I had to interact with staff.

We had heard from other travelers that the dinners at Bullara are fabulous. We arranged our travels around their special dinner with matching wines from Oates End complete with the wonderful winemaker, Cath. From the start with a personal welcome from Edwina who owns the property with her husband Tim to the superb eye fillet beef with incredible sauce and cheesy garlic potatoes in camp oven, the amazing dessert and everything in between this will be a night we will remain a highlight of our travels. Do book ahead though as they book out quickly. The pies, scones and coffee were great too. We were glad they sold their beans as we needed some more and now we can enjoy Bullara blend in our onward travels. Showers were spacious, hot and clean. Our dog loved the different well marked walks around the property. We highly recommend a stay here.
Must see.  Food great.  Potable water. Great place to relax. You can day trip to Coral Bay instead of staying there and getting ripped off.

what a great vibe , stayed for 2 nights and could have stayed longer . Great camping ,big sites, water was good , can’t go past the open air quirky showers so much better than a small mouldy cubicle in a caravan park 🤪🤦‍♀️.  The restaurant had a good menu and the pies looked amazing . lots to look at do. They sell there own cryvac steak in the check-in to take away and it was delicious. Best station stay yet .

Loved our 5 nights stay here. Great experience with friendly and helpful staff. Scones and pies were too good, with 1 of each nearly every day stayed 🤫. Pizza and Burger night also great with good vibes. Also a good base to explore Coral Bay and Exmouth. Well done Bullara 👍
Scones were good but the Bullara pie oh my goodness next level so so good best pie on our trip actually best pie in the last 2 years

Absolutely brilliant. We will be back. Hamburger night was great. Staff were excellent and so friendly. Live Entertainment as well.
You could have a quiet board game in the Cafe/bar area. Plus fresh home made  pies and sausage rolls.
Very unique environment. Lots to see and do as the owners/workers have put so much thought into this place. Little things like tin can wall and sunset walk. Just a bloody Amazing place.
Nice experience kids had lost of Fun. Water is also available on non powered sites

We stayed here 2 nights and loved it.
Very quirky and rustic. All the staff were amazing. We will be back one day. 😊
You don't want to miss Bullara Station during your travels! We were there during the epic pizza and burger nights. Thank you Dave and Sue, you made us feel so welcome.
Best scones ever!!!

We loved our time at Bullara Station. Firstly we must commend you on your wonderful setup. The laid back vibe and great staff are a credit to you. Having power and fresh water hookup was great, the quality of your water is outstanding. My husband said that he has never tasted water like yours anywhere! So we'll done Bullara!!
Having come from a farming background, the use of all your farming memorabilia gems is wonderful to see.
We put our bottle onto the bottle tree, tried the pies and had the scones, jam and cream twice! Burger night was great and flowed effortlessly, it shows that you do it each week. We also did the sunset, windmill and tin can walks and thoroughly enjoyed our stay at Bullara Station.

Absolutely loved our time at Bullara we were there 2 nights and could easily have stayed weeks. The kids had a great time seeing the animals, especially Billy the Goat. Pizza and burger nights are a must do. All staff were very friendly and welcoming and the treats at the cafe were amazing.

Ok stay for 2 nights ( Thursday / Friday ) Site for rear fold camper was adequate, showers and toilets did the job with low pressured showers sometimes which is to be expected with numerous people using others. Pizza night was ok , burger night was very confusing, on booking was stated to be there at a certain time and would be called up in groups however it's just a free for all jumping in line.
Enjoyed the 2 days but won't be rushing back.

We stayed in the bell tents which were well set out but we could hear both the highway and the generator which drowned out the early morning bird song! Great atmosphere in the woodshed bar and long table dinner.
Only called in for a coffee and a Pie, wasnt disappointed, would definitely come back to stay.
Great atmosphere. Amazing toilets and showers. Burger night was a hit. So many interesting sculptures and buildings etc. lots to see and do around the area too

Great stay, we had the Cabott Cottage.
Superb food from Scones, carrot cake to Pies and the dinner.
Very good meat to purchase for cooking.
Overall great place.

We stayed four nights at Bullara Station and absolutely loved every minute. It’s such an easy place to unwind—laid-back, welcoming, and never too busy. Whether you’re relaxing by the firepit, wandering the grounds, or just soaking in the vibe, it’s the kind of place that makes you instantly feel at home.

The food was a real standout. The pastries from the café were fresh, buttery, and ridiculously good—easily some of the best we’ve had on the road. And the gourmet dinners? Next level. Each night we were blown away by the flavour, creativity, and quality of the meals. Who knew you could eat like this in the middle of the outback?

Bullara nails that perfect mix of rustic charm, genuine hospitality, and top-notch food. Can’t recommend it highly enough.

We had the most amazing experience at Bullara, amazing service and perfect for pet friendly camping. We loved the trails and the beautiful artisan showrers!  Would recommend Bullara to everyone and we will definitely be back!
Wer die Chance hat, sollte unbedingt vorbei kommen! So liebevoll und authentisch. Café und Bar auch super lecker. Ein besonderes Erlebnis. Sehr sauber

Bullara Station was such a lovely place to stay! We spent two nights here and loved it. The camp kitchen was fantastic, there were some great walks to explore, and it was the perfect spot to relax. The bar had a fun, welcoming vibe, and the scones… absolutely delicious (and huge!). Definitely a stop everyone should add to their trip.

What an experience! Staying at Bullara Station was the highlight of our camping trip. We stayed on a powered site so didn’t see the accommodation but the place has everything you need. The outdoor showers were very nice, the bar is a need after a long drive and was busy on an evening which created a nice vibe whilst everyone sat around the fire pit at night. All the staff were lovely too. We stayed on a Thursday which was pizza night, where a company bring in their own pizza oven and if you don’t fancy cooking I would 100% recommend getting one of their pizzas it was fab! You have to stay here it’s fantastic!

We loved our stay here. So relaxing and calming. We walked, we lazed about, we ate scones, it was all lush!

There are a variety of nice walks to do and the location is good for exploring Cape Range and Exmouth or Coral Bay. We did not do the 4WD track to the gulf as our vehicle didn’t have good clearance but would love to do it if we came again.

We “cheated” and stayed in one of the cottages which was beautifully appointed and really comfortable.

The facilities were excellent with washing machines available, a good range of meat available at reception and the lovely cafe and bar.

Do take the opportunity to go stargazing with Paul if you are there on a Monday - he was fabulous and we had an amazing time. We also ate at Table on one of the nights and loved the food - excellent quality and really lovely to chat to fellow guests.

We left thoroughly relaxed and if we didn’t live on the other side of the world, we’d be back regularly!

My favourite station stay. Wonderful relaxed environment. Lots of quirky details like the lava tree open top tank showers, roaming sheep, goats and cattle. Dog friendly. Pre book dinner a month before or have a snack platter and a cocktail.

Best place I’ve stayed on my WA trip & was a last minute add in. Everything was incredible!!! Did not want to leave and would highly recommend. Pies chef kiss and scones were really good (I’m from the U.K. and it’s hard to come by a good scone in aus and this was pretty bang on).

Huge and full. Wonderful style, rustic modern. Well maintained. Facilities normally only found at resorts. Excellent Cafe, great coffee, scones jam and cream, pies, sausage rolls. Unfortunately, needed to book weeks in advance for dinner, apparently good.
Great spot great sconces

What a treat to emerge from a week of bush camping to arrive at Bullara - it offers a quirky experience (a ‘Lava Tree’ shower is a true bush experience 😉), nice campsites, fire pits, excellent scones and pies, a bar, washing machines and great hospitality. I was only sad I did not organise to have a meal at The Table! Station Stays are an excellent way to support these hard working hosts who have diversified to welcome weary travellers👍
Amazing pies, good coffe great environment and styling with love! Next time will buy some meat!

Stayed two nights at a powered site, swagging it . Did the Bullara beef festival and burger night , two night stay.
Absolutely had a ripper weekend, staff were amazing and welcoming , food was 5star , amenities were clean , functional and had station character.
Definitely be back.
Fantastic stay, burger and pizza night was awesome. Friendly staff and very helpful. Wonderful coffee and scones. A must do stay. Don't miss it.

Great stopover for a few days. Generous sized sites.  Staff are friendky and super helpful Licenced restaurant, bar and Cafe on site. Live music at the bar of an afternoon. Locally produced fresh meat for sale. Absolutely recommend

Amazing place with a really cool vibe! We stayed in a powered site so I can’t speak for the rooms/lodges but the amenities, cafe and restaurant and staff were incredible. It was our favourite place to stay on a two week long ningaloo road trip and we will definitely be back. The restaurant also catered to our dietary requirements kindly, and the scones are amazing! The outdoor showers and toilets are very clean and insect/animal free (which was something I was worried about before attending!) and the staff were very polite and helpful. Wish we could have stayed longer!
The best station stay around. You must do this one if you're travelling the area.
What an amazing place to stay. Facilities are great. Lots to see and do around the station. Loved the fact the big bull just wanders about freely. Easy drive to the coast for day trips.

Bullara station is a great Australian example of station life and we have been fortunate to enjoy staying on a couple if occasions.
This is one of the best organised campgrounds without imposing on the guests. The daily operations just run smoothly whilst we can enjoy the outstanding cafe (try the pies!), the many walks or just marvel at the farm.
The big treat us having dinner at The Long Table - if you've been lucky to book months ahead. Chef Toby and his wife Maree will create something to tempt the best of appetites. Their meat is also for sale and tender.
As a visitor from Queensland, Bullara station is our trip's highlight. Do yourself a favour but book early.

Everything positive you read about this place is spot on
Lots of time and money has been spent to make this a memorable stay.
Friendly and knowledgeable staff, large sites (many drive-through), quirky facilities (some with trees in the middle of the shower and toilet) outstanding pies, sausage rolls and their famous scones, jam and cream.  But get there early, they do sell out!
The bar (from 4pm) stocks a great range of beers, wines and spirits that can be had inside or outside around the fire pit, and the restaurant is a must during your stay
Highly recommended
A must do-it-yourself in the area, we loved it and will return.

What a vibe! Getting escorted to our campsite via a bike volunteer was a cool quirky touch. The kids games/bmx track and outdoor showers was the kids favourite. The entertainment and food and drink that was provided was five star and the highlight of our trip this is the station we talk about the most to our friends.

A really polarising stay! In general, lovely and well cared for campground deserving of the hype. Great showers and facilities. But we were let down  on a few occasions by the service, or lack of care.

Would absolutely not recommend the burger night if you have any dietary requirements. We were welcomed when we made our booking to join and bring our own veggie burgers, and we were a bit bemused that we had to pay the full price despite only having the bread and salad (so more including the $10 on veggie burgers we brought) but went with it and expected to enjoy the night. We arrived early, and gave our burgers to staff to be cooked, although they told us they’d do them after all the meat was finished instead of before. We double checked a couple of times when it seemed like the majority of cow eaters had been served, and were told not to worry and someone would come and get us. Nobody did, and 30 mins later (after 2 hours waiting) we asked staff about our food, staff who at this point had all eaten themselves and had mostly clocked off. A lady looked under a bbq hood and there were many leftover meat burgers wrapped in foil, but she couldn’t find ours (which are hard to distinguish by eye from flesh ones). She looked concerned and then left and shortly after came back with another man who’d found our burgers, which had long since been cooked and were sat cold, in the meat tray. The man made a joke about it being a bit pointless having veggie burgers cooked on the grill after the meat and then sat in beef fat! So the choice was a cold and contaminated burger, or go back to our camp and cook despite it being 8:30 by this point. We opted for the former and took some of the long past best dregs of salad.

The next day we were leaving and asked for our money back for the burger night. The office staff initially seemed resistant, but after a short wait the owner came and spoke to us. She spent a few mins with us and was very accommodating and apologetic and offered a refund for the burger night, asking us to wait and the team would arrange it. After leaving we waited..and waited…and waited… after 45 mins sat ready to leave we asked and the office team weren’t aware we were waiting, but told us they thought we’d already left and would automatically arrange our refund! So between this and the night before’s burger chaos the breakdowns in comms between the team really let us and then down.

Another example - the scones and cafe area were nice, but the server was quite unfriendly.
The pizza night was fantastic, but the music was pretty abysmal and barely karaoke quality.

So all in all a really mixed bag. Brought up a star by the owner’s attentiveness but our lasting memory is that we definitely wouldn’t return and if we did, would stay well clear of burger night!

We’d not known about the station so had planned on breaking our trip at Nanaturra - I called in the off chance of there being a spare space , left a message and never expecting a reply but ….. voila - they called us back at around 15:15 and we were in!! What a wonderful experience. Fabulous hospitality from checkin to showing us our site. The atmosphere at the Woolshed in the evening for drinks was perfect and then of course the mandatory scones, jam and cream with a coffee for breakfast - just delicious. We also bought Bullard  beef pies which we thoroughly enjoyed for dinner that night. Do not miss this place. PS If you want dinner you’d need to book well in advance.. so popular.
Fantastic camping experience
This is a great place to stay. Helpful, friendly staff. Great food and coffee. Fascilities are eclectic yet imaculately clean. Cant reccomend highly enough. We will be back for sure.

Good size unpowered site.
Great location and friendly staff and service.
excellent little place to stay.
Awesome. Great facilities and staff. Great food.  Was central to Coral Bay and Exmouth.

Wonderful stop over between Exmouth and Coral Bay on our road trip. We made it for burger night and it was such an experience. Better still were the scones and pies (we took for our next road trip) the next day. Definitely check in here, wished we had more time. Will be back for a long table dinner hopefully.

We had a wonderful stay at Bullara station. We camped at a powered site, I was actually on crutches but that didn’t stop us. The facilities (showers, toilets, etc) were excellent, they were clean & spacious. Dinner at the table was enjoyable. There was live music and the kids loved the communal fires and playground. It was a fun family experience. Edwina was delightful and we look forward to returning again soon. The staff are knowledgeable and if they don’t know they’ll find out for you.

An awesome stay. The Bullarra team have done a great job of creating an authentic relaxed camping experience. Woodshed cafe, bar, ablution amenities and service are so well done. Great location near Exmouth and fabulous beaches and coastline  - a million times nicer than the awful cheek to jowel caravan parks in nearby Coral Bay and Exmouth.
super friendly staff and really well thought out. you need to book in advance as this spot gets super busy.
The best station stay. We stayed with our caravan. Fantastic atmosphere. The best scones ever. Great restaurant.

Had the best experience. Was not expecting the facilities to be that clean considering it’s a farm and it’s half outdoor .
People were really nice , food was incredible
Would recommend

Tout était très bien, il y a pas mal de choses à voir dans le camping, des balades à faire, des animaux etc…

L’équipe et l’organisation était parfaite.

Notre seul problème a été que nous avons été mis sur un emplacement de camping entre 2 emplacements dont les occupants se connaissaient.
Les gens de ces emplacements passaient donc en plein dans notre emplacement à 20cm de notre véhicule pour se déplacer entre leurs 2 emplacements sans même nous regarder où s’excuser. Ils auraient dû faire le tour et dire à leurs enfants de ne pas passer en courant et en criant sur notre emplacement.
Nous mangions dehors sur notre emplacement et devions partager cet espace avec des enfants et des adultes très bruyants.

Ces gens là ne respectaient clairement pas l’espace que nous avons payé, c’est pourtant écrit dans le règlement de la station.

Nous ne pouvons pas en vouloir aux employés ou à la station pour cela car c’est uniquement de la faute de ces familles mais nous devons avouer que ça nous a en grande partie gâcher notre séjour à Bullara Station Stay.
Nearly 2 months in busy season and still charge cancelation, greedy and why I don't recommend.

Loved this place! If you want a really great farm experience came to Bullara Station it has it all. Live music, great dinners,  awesome facilities and quirky as all get-out. We will be back and will stay longer next time.
Fantastic station stay 😊 friendly staff 😊 nice big sites 😊 dog friendly 😊 great food from cafe (scones are to die for) 😊 must buy some meat from reception 😊🙃🙃
Amazing staff, location and people. Clean showers and toilets, staff keep it tip top.

Outstanding!
Very busy during school holidays... obviously. Great place for kids!
Great stay for 2 nights. Such a vibe and the hospitality was top notch. Food amazing and such a cool place to hang out for a few nights - burger night is a must!
This is an Outback Station caravan park. 140 sites so it's big but well managed. I recommend staying at least 2 nights.
The whole station experience is well worth the stay, especially attending the Burger night, so many people
Great place to stay with great facilities, as well as their restaurant being a highlight of the stay

Friendly welcoming staff, spacious sites among the trees, kid friendly activities, live music, quirky amenities. We enjoyed the coffee and scones. You can tell that a lot of love and passion has gone into giving the guests an exceptional experience. Water also fantastic, fill your tanks up.

We spent a couple of nights in Bullara's campground and it's by far the best one in the Exmouth area. Incredible vibes and atmosphere. The location is lovely, stylish and at the same time clean and with all the amenities you may need. Lots of activities during the week and one breakfast at the café is a must (the home made scones, pies and sausage rolls are amazing). Overall definitely recommended spending at least two nights to fully enjoy the experience.
What an amazing place and a sensational experience. The team were excellent, the facilities were incredible. This is a must do stay.

Our family of four stayed at the Safari Tents at Bullara Station. The tents were so well kitted out and had everything we needed, but didn’t stop us heading up to the woodshed cafe for some delicious coffee and cake and lunch options. The pies are so delicious! We also had dinner at the Table one night which was restaurant quality food served in the iconic shearing shed - an amazing experience.
There is plenty to see and do at Bullara and it far from your normal station stay so would highly recommend.
Very good old cattle station great staff great food great fun

Cannot recommend this Station Stay highly enough, it is a working cattle station (cattle are out grazing the enormous pastures within the station so you won’t get to see them around the campground.  It has good amenities, has the famous donkey showers plus regular showers around the 160 campsites.  The campsites are all exceptionally large (almost twice the size of normal cp sites).  Recommend booking early as it’s always busy especially if you have a large setup.  There are only 60 powered site but all sites offer fresh drinking water and there is a dump point.

There are various things to see and do around the station and you can buy firewood from reception on arrival and it will be delivered between 4pm and 5pm to your site, they also sell their beef from fridges in reception but get there early as they sell out quickly, best steak and not too badly priced either.  The cafe and on the station do the yummiest pies, sausage rolls and scones as well as other things.  The restaurant there is awesome and the food is tasty (not cheap) but so yummy again see what they are cooking on the nights you’re there as it gets booked out just as quickly as the campsites do.
Great sites, beautiful sunsets, relic tractors and a wonderful vibe.
The Woolshed cafe is great! Delicious pastries, scones and coffee and I loved the decor. The staff are so friendly and we loved our stay.
Stopped here for lunch at cafe, beautiful station will visit and stay next time up here😍

Just wow.  What else can I say.  We stayed in a Safari Hut.   $450 per night sounds expensive but we’ve paid double that for a Glamping Experience nowhere near as good.  The station is busy but vast so doesn’t feel over crowded.  Booked ourselves in to The Table for dinner each night.  Two nights in and the food has been sensational.  Very lucky to have such a talented chef on site.  Tried the scones from the bakery and bought some burgers for cooking, OMG, both awesome.  The bar is a great way to start the night, sitting by the fire with a glass of wine. Camp Host Dave is very informative and helped us prepare for the 4WD track to the gulf, glad we did as we loved it.  We’ve been visiting loads of places and chatting with people they consistently said Bullara was the best.  It is.  It’s magnificent.  I would stay here for months in a heartbeat.  Although we are en suite we had to check out the outdoor toilets and showers.  Last but not least just come and witness the teamwork between the staff.  It feels like a massive family.  This place is Bucket List Worthy.  Well done.
You have never had scones before that taste as good as Bullaras. No need to stay just pull in and head to the cafe. No regrets....

What a great experience , from the time we arrived the reception staff were knowledgeable and helpful and naturally nice . The camp host makes sure you’re settled and comfortable . Pizza night , burger night , scones , fresh station meat . Make sure you book for those nights as it is very popular . Open showers , big sites  Bullara is well located only 60 to Coral Bay and 100 to Exmouth . We left Bullara for a few days at Exmouth which we loved but for those worried about some dust at Bullara station plenty  of that on our premium site at Exmouth -   No wonder everyone we go people talk about Bullara station

Fantastic good everything
Clean facilities
Safe place
We go there when ever go up there

Charming place..
Good Coffee and pie’s.

This place is incredible. Facilities are extremely clean and well maintained. Super hot showers! Such a treat in a place like this. Staff are lovely and we have had many calls to check in and advise us of dinner options.
My only criticism is the fly screen doors on the shearer’s lodge rooms. They slam EXTREMELYYY loudly. It’s not ideal when you have kids with an early bedtime. But, a lot of things aren’t ideal when you travel with kids 😅 we will be back!

What a rip off,do not believe website,ther is no pool,rude reception,nice food but overpriced by 50% compared to anywhere else,no entertainment while we were there.absolutely nothing to do after you’ve done the walk.Stay in Exmouth ,much better value.

The camp ground is beautiful in a rusty way for the outback station. We stayed 2 nights and loved it, never thought an outdoor shower can be so peaceful to shower in. My wife said the coffee and scones are delicious.
Pulled in here on the way to Coral Bay for morning tea. Pleasantly surprised. The photos tell it all. The famous scones with jam and cream are a must.
Second visit this place is truly worth a stay
Sehr schöner und rustikaler Campingplatz mit kleinem Laden wo frisches Fleisch und selbstgemachte Produkte gekauft werden können.

From the moment we arrived, the staff were incredibly warm and welcoming – you can really feel the heart and soul that goes into this place. The Woodshed Café was a daily highlight – the freshly baked scones with jam and cream were out of this world, and the coffee was top quality too!

In the evenings, the bar opened at 4pm – perfect for relaxing with a drink and meeting other travellers. But the real standout was The Table, Bullara’s own restaurant. Every dish was packed with flavour, using only the freshest local ingredients – true farm-to-fork dining in the best way possible.

The whole experience is just something special – a genuine outback atmosphere with creature comforts, great food, and the loveliest people. We couldn’t recommend Bullara Station more highly. A must-stop if you’re travelling through the region!

Hate to say it,...reminded me of a camp ground with trees around it in a quarry!  I was looking forward to staying here but have to say slightly disappointing. Lots of quirky bits & pieces which were very interesting & they have a lot of different  options for a good feed which did seem a little expensive,.but worth the experience?.  But I have to say  very dusty & hot when we were there. . So when the power keeps tripping out because too many people on same grid,..that does nothing to impress me. You know about the problem but fail to warn anyone before they arrive.Even your staff told us that the generator was old & did not like the heat. It had been a problem for some time ,  especially the last few days when it had dropped out. Fix the problem. For what you charge for a powered site ,I would expect the power to be a little more reliable & to be relying on guests ( I witnessed that with my own eyes) to advise you of power tripping seems ridiculous ,especially as often as it occurred.
The pies here are fantastic!

Wow!
I don't even know where to start!
Loved everything about Bullara Station!
It was our first time staying here and will definitely not be our last!
We loved the Bull coming to visit everyone and the sheep just wandering around!
The open bathrooms were a real treat too.
The staff were all fantastic.
Very cool to see the Brahman's everywhere including on the road just watching us when we drove out to the Gulf. One young one we met was very entertaining with his zoomies.
I was also so impressed with how clean everywhere is, there wasn't even a cobweb in sight, amenities were outstanding every time!
We also enjoyed a very delicious meal at "The Table" and thoroughly enjoyed the social side of it too making new friends!
Thank you so much for creating such an amazing, friendly enjoyable quirky place everyone needs to see!
See you soon Tez & Geoff
Love the cowboy vibe! Perfect stop before exmouth

This place is AMAZING!
Run so well, dozens of staff around who will take you to your camp.
More than 100 campsites, over half have power and water.
The cafe/bar/restaurant is open daily and several evenings a week serve excellent meals - one choice, 3 courses, and can cope with dietary issues and allergies.
Lunches available and great scones!
Plenty of rustic, unique, clean toilets and showers.
Highly recommended. 5/5

Such a special place! Well worth the drive out. We stayed for just one night (wish it was longer) and enjoyed some awesome hospitality in a carefully considered community space. Be sure to book in for the evening meal; the food was exquisite made up of ingredients thoughtfully sourced. 10:10 recommend.

Had a night stay at the camp ground. Place itself was nice,I like their bar/ dining area has a rustic design.friendly staff.
Also you can buy a nice coffee and freshly baked sausage rolls and pies.
I cannot rate the room because we did not stay there.
It was a worth one night stay heading to coral bay.

What a fantastic place! They've created a great outback experience here. The atmosphere is great, with a real community feel. The outdoor showers are a must, as are the scones and live entertainment. We didn't eat at the restaurant as we had our kids. Lots of ablutions available and full of character. Worth a visit.

Bullara station is such a unique and incredible place. The pies and scones are to die for, lots of animals for the kids to see, licensed venue on site to sit back and relax and have a wine. Plus the outdoor showers are amazing. Our only upset was they have caravan sites literally right next to the toilets that over half the park is using. We were less an 2m from the toilets and could hear everyone doing their business, plus the big heavy metal doors banged away all night with people using them waking us and our 2 year old up. We did ask to move but was told we could not. Next time we will request specifically to be away from the toilet block. We will for sure be back!

Huge sites which is nice for a change.
It is a busy place, people coming & going, but the nights are quiet.
We had the set menu dinner our first night, which was nice. The meal was delicious.

My only gripe was the service I received from the staff member in the cafe. I am only assuming he was the chef (?).
I knew the cafe was closed & I understood that, however the door was not shut & I popped my head in just to ask what time the scones would be available the next morning…
He was not at all happy with me & came at me shooing me away yelling that it was closed! Good grief. I explained that I only had a question, but not to worry & proceeded to leave the area. He came after me asking what I wanted to know. I told him… needless to say I chose not to come back for scones.
Great place, good walking tracks, Great food served in the restaurant. And an ideal place to do day trips to Exmouth and Coral Bay.

Wir waren (leider) nur eine Nacht dort, aber hatten definitiv einen sehr schönen Aufenthalt. Die Mitarbeiter sind alle super freundlich und man kommt schnell ins Gespräch:)
Das Restaurant/Bar/Cafe sind sehr schön gemacht und die Leckereien zu empfehlen!
Busy crowded popular station stay along the main access road into Exmouth. A bit too crowded for my tastes but with their cafe and always fully booked restaurant its a good spot to camp.

Brilliant spot to stay, we had a safari tent, and it was really well equipped. The staff are lovely and the woodshed was a great spot to enjoy the chefs artwork.

Highly recommend 👌
We were put in a tight site. With young children everywhere. Not that we mind children.  But climbing over our car coming  into our camp-site unattended  we have two dogs. So wasn't too happy.

Outstanding food - the cafe pumps out nothing but quality, we tried lots of things and everything was incredibly delicious, we also had the long table dinners which were amazing, pizza night with live music was brilliant.
Outdoor showers were magic, all the team working here are so lovely and helpful, keep up the sensational work Bullara!!!
Absolutely what a place we loved it🥰

An amazing station stay - they’ve got it all covered here. Restaurant, cafe, bar, great amenities, lovely friendly atmosphere and amazing sunsets. If you’re coming past this way, definitely stay a night or two and just let yourself relax🤗
I absolutely loved stay at Bullara. A real Australian outback experience.  I would love to return in the future.

What a ripper of a station stay! Highly recommended! Have a shower under the sun, or moon for that matter. Go get a feed at the Table, or check out the food from the cafe. It's all bonkers good. Try the Bullara beef pie. It rocked.
Awesome experience, fairly pricey however great setup.

What a great stay! The burgers lived up to the hype, the staff were fun and friendly and the vibe was relaxed. We stayed in our own van, but saw the safari tents, which were fabulous. 100% would stay here again.
Stayed 1 night but wish it was longer. Great atmosphere, friendly animals and nice walking tracks.

Was recommended to visit here but didn’t expect it to be so busy. If you are looking for quiet/bush/station experience then this isn’t for you.  Quite a lot of noise came  from other camping families next to us throughout our stay. Owners have done an amazing job with modern features - water, power, toilets, showers, camp kitchen, cafe, bar, dining, signposted walking trails. Staff were mostly friendly and helpful.
Loved all the quirky artefacts around the park like the bottle tree and we even  had a bull come to visit us!
We stayed for two nights and it was very hot so not much to do and everyone seemed to hibernate in their vans or shade. A pool or waterhole for a dip would make the stay perfect. Quite a return drive to swim at Coral Bay or Exmouth.
As we were there on Saturday and Sunday we were able to enjoy pizza night and dining at the table. Both really good though the table meal seemed a bit overpriced for what we got. Enjoyed chatting with other guests. It was also Mother’s Day so was surprised that they did nothing special to celebrate this for the mums present. 😕
The scones from the cafe were delicious.
Lots of flies and moths - don’t forget your fly net.

Sadly would not recommend if you have any type of food allergies. Restaurant was booked out and pizza van could not provide gluten free. Asked 3 different members of staff if any food at allcould be provided… staff were very far from helpful, only recommendation was to drive 50 mins for food. When explain staff there was no food atall I could eat staff memeber said ‘poor thing’.Lovely place but unfortunately staff so unhelpful from reception to waitress.

Had an absolute ball staying at Bullara Station Stay.
I would highly recommend staying here for at least a few days, if not longer.
Pizza night, hamburger night,and the "long table" dinner were all delicious. Oh and the famous scones were scrumptious.

Excellent experience, exceeds the hype and expectations. Booked into the Table for the Saturday dinner, great experience serviced family style at large tables with other guests - food was a amazing and very generous. Campground was safe and secure with excellent toilet and showers dotted around the area. The bar area is open after 4:30pm and is great for a sundowner. The coffee and scones were delicious the next morning. Highly highly recommend staying here.

Naja, was soll ich sagen… Die Nacht auf der Bullara Station war leider nicht das, was wir uns erhofft hatten. Unsere Vorstellung: Ruhe, Sternenhimmel, wir als Familie und das echte Outback-Gefühl. Die Realität: Massenandrang, laute Gruppen und eine Stimmung, die eher an ein Festival erinnerte als an einen Rückzugsort in der Natur. Schade!!!

I checked out this place on YouTube and read google reviews. Yes, the pies are glorious. The scones are so light and fluffy covered in cream and jam. The staff are so helpful. This place knows how to treat customers with hosts guiding us to our campsites. The showers amongst the lava trees are an instagram delight. Livestock wander around the property looking for someone to pat them. Talk about rustic with reused and upcycled farm machinery turned into art installations, door handles, showers, light stands...the list goes on. Then there are the communal areas where you have the chance to meet up with other travellers and share a yarn over a cold beer. There are too many positives to describe about this place.
Amazing scones and sausage rolls.

I only stopped in for a snack but this is the best pie I’ve ever had in my entire life and I reckon I’ve had a lot of pies. I don’t think I’ll ever stop thinking about this pie, I’d travel back from the other side of the country again just to buy this pie.

Just look at it.
Incredible.
Steak pie ♥️

Incredible place!

Stayed two nights. Was super hot but the staff kept checking on everyone and were in high spirits.

The friday burger night was a highlight of the holiday! Don’t miss out.

Enjoyed the outback walks and excellent cafe.

Overall super happy with our stay and would love to go back next time we are in WA. All staff were excellent and such a great vibe.
We stayed in a shearer's lodge and it was all good. The whole place is great and interestingly genuine as a station. Lots to look at, good meals to be had at the "pub", lovely people. The best!
Had the privilege of enjoying a coffee and scone, met some great staff members. Unfortunately no pies as it was there opening day for the season today.

Bullara Station is a fantastic and authentic outback experience! This is a true station stay, and it was amazing to encounter sheep and cattle handling up close—such a unique experience that really connects you to the land. The station has a rustic charm, with quirky restaurant and shower designs that add to the atmosphere. We were lucky to be there for burger night, which was a fun and memorable experience even though the burger wasn’t gourmet—it’s the vibe and setting that make it special. Highly recommend Bullara for anyone looking for a genuine, one-of-a-kind station stay!

Honestly can’t rate Bullara highly enough.

We stayed for four nights, including the burger night, which had a real wholesome, family type feel, with live music and burgers direct from the station.

The staff were spot on, super friendly and everyone seemed to genuinely care about the station. There are even little walks mapped out, including a sunset walk that you can do from the campsite, goats roaming free and wood fire pizzas available to takeaway back to your camp - they’ve thought of everything!

The owners here have really done something magical with the place and we felt really blessed to have stumbled across it!

Will definitely stay again!
Most amazing station I’ve been to. Excellent all round. Could not fault!!
Working cattle station with accomodation and caravan sites. Very popular pizza and hamburger nights, best scones jam and cream. Rustic
Quirky fun place.  You have to explore to see it.  Bar and Cafe.  I would stay again.

We stayed in our caravan for a week, and enjoyed the restaurant with good coffee and scones. Also within close proximity to Coral bay.
Did not enjoy all the dust in the campsites, and the close proximity of some campers, especially loud younger ones and noisy kids We recommend setting certain campsites and areas aside for older couples who value their peace and quiet.

Such rude people
They needed to find an excuse to kick us out even though we paid to eat and to stop for a couple hours to avoid the heat. We could have done some good feedback to friends etc.
Very disappointing and sad when australian are against tourists/backpackers and treat them like thiefs

What a quirky station to stay at. Camp host are very friendly & they even show you to your campsite. Spacious sites, cows & sheep wondering around freely. We stayed for burger night which was awesome the atmosphere & just the whole setup. Staff did a fantastic job.

Tim & Edwina thank you both for letting us experience Bullara Station. It was the highlight of our road trip.
The staff are so helpful & happy obviously loving there jobs at Bullara.
The food was amazing from the scones to the famous burger night & the fresh meat to purchase in reception.
Awesome live music with drinks from the bar at reasonable prices.
We were so lucky to be there when the muster was on we honestly enjoyed every minute of it.
We were the crazy ones sitting there for 3 days in the elements watching on.
Big shout out to Eddie what a character with a great sense of humour & many laughs.
We will be recommending Bullara Station to everyone. It is definitely our favorite place to stay in WA 10 out of 10.
Bullara Station gives you a true outback experience you can't get more aussie than that.

We can't wait to come back
Jacqui & Simon
Love dust, stay here 😀. Busy station stay, reasonable prices too.

We had an amazing experience at Bullara station, we stayed in the beautiful shearers shed and the amenities were easy to access with a dedicated communal kitchen, fridge and living area and attached ablutions. It was “glamping” vibes but tastefully done. We were lucky enough to be there for a Friday night burger night, which were delicious and really big with live entertainment and communal area too set up camp chairs to listen. With plenty of people joining around the fire in the evening.

The sunset was beautiful and would definitely go back and highly recommend if you have never been. It’s a pity it’s so far from Perth but a hidden gem none the less.

For the 4WD enthusiasts, the gulf track about 1KM before the entrance to the left (coming from coral bay/exmouth) is a great route and the end point is the mangroves with the seas just spectacular. Guests can sign into the gulf track route near the kitchens to ensure if you aren’t back someone will know to look for you.
The track can be quite sandy at points so keep an eye on your vehicle, we lost a spotty to this track.
Incredible place, not expensive with great facilities, food and staff, loved it

Great place for some different when touring up north. Spacious sites for vans, great interactive cafe/restaurant, excellent coffee & scones. Fantastic for family's. Seeing is believing. Fantastic location to leave the van & enjoy day trips to explore Ningaloo coast, Exmouth & the likes 👍
Great stay! Relaxed environment with great coffee and pies!

Sehr liebevoll eingerichtet und organisiert. In der Mitte zwischen exmouth und Coral bay. Sogar walks sind ausgeschildert. Konzerte am Abend. Rustikale Einrichtung. Trinkwasser....alles was man braucht
Toll! Super toll! Ein Stück australisches Kulturgut!

Big disappointment
Admittedly, the location and amenities are original and totally in keeping with the Western theme of the campsite. The place is nice BUT the staff are not at all commercial.
First of all, the welcome wasn't very warm. When we arrived, we told the person at reception that our van's fridge had broken down, and she sold us a bag of ice for $10.50... instead of telling us that the fridge and freezer were available FREE of charge at the campsite (which we found out for ourselves afterwards).
Then we absolutely had to wash our sheets, already noticing that there were only washing machines and no dryers available... we still asked for some change at reception and at the bar in order to have some coins to make our machine, but as we didn't have any notes with us, everyone refused to give us any change, even though now in 2024 it's very easy to change money with a bank card... another disappointment on the human side
I would also like to point out that the campsite restaurant is full very quickly, and it's a shame that the service isn't adapted to the number of people on the campsite.
These are all the points that made my stay at this campsite a disappointment after having been tempted to come here following videos on social networks.
It really isn't a must on any trip to Exmouth.
It's up to you to make up your own mind, and I hope you have a great experience, unlike mine.
Have a nice day!
Beautiful place 10/10, don't miss burger night !

Fantastic place to stay with caravan with great atmosphere and facilities. Staff are really committed to the vision of the owners and very approachable. The only downside is the minority of dog owners who are happy to let their dogs yap and bark and watch their dogs defecate on other sites but not clean up after them.

Dining experience was great .
Scones were cold and a bit dry .
Chia pudding was great

What an absolutely amazing place!
We spent 5 days there and loved every minute.
Great staff who, by the way, clearly loved working there. They were all so cheerful and helpful.
The scones. You’ve just gotta try them!
The day we arrived was burger night. Timing impeccable. They were fresh and tasty.
We had to change up our holiday a bit due to car issues. Bullara didn’t initially have booking space for us. Our stay was originally only 2 nights. When we arrived we asked again. They were brilliant.
They managed to work it so we could stay an extra 3 nights so we could get our car sorted.
The walk trails were great as well.
Thank you to all the staff at Bullara.
We will be back!!!

Pre booked stay 2 Adults in August split across powered 4n & unpowered 3n due to Burger night (Fridays) being booked out well in advance.Amazing place, great staff at every interaction, smoothest check in , with sizeable  waiting bays, terrific reception and a camp host to show you to site.
There are walks through the bush and to the lookout, all clearly signed/marked and a lovely way to explore some of the property, as was the trip to the gulf on the 4WD track, rather corrugated in many places but an interesting drive.
We enjoyed the scones, pies, coffee and milkshakes from the cafe across different days and all lived up to their publicised reputation.  As did burger night, 420 served the night we attended, so a wait was inevitable, but the queue kept moving and there was plenty of food for all. The bar is licensed from 4:30pm and has a range of craft beers as well as wine with service to your table if required. Bush poets, live music and the history of the station were all worth going to the woodshed for, they added to the enjoyment of our stay.
Amenities are large, somewhat quirky and always clean. Laundry has two coin operated machines with plenty of available drying lines.
Overall is was a great place to stay, if you’re in the area it’s worth the visit.

A great place to camp for a few days. Loved the insights to station life. Good 4WD drive track on the station down to the coast. Lovely walks. Lots of active birdlife on the station. Sunset Hill was really worthwhile! And the station had a Cafe which serves scones & coffee etc, then good meals in the evenings if you feel like it.
These guys have got a great vibe going . Had a awesome feed and laugh at the burger night. 10/10🤘

We were escorted to our caravan site by a host on his bicycle. The nearby toilets and showers were quite rustic and novel. We booked the evening set menu meal. Sirloin steak, vegetables and delicious dessert. Great value and a good way to meet people.
Café was very nice and had an authentic, classic regional Australian feel to it. Enjoyed scones, carrot cake and coffee on the tables outside in the shade.

I hadn't heard of this station stay but it was recommended to us by so many along our north west trip.
Unfortunately our 2 week in advance spur of the trip booking meant we couldn't get in on Thursday, Friday or Saturday night so we missed out on Friday Burger night and the weekend entertainment. The Table restaurant also fully booked Sunday to Wednesday nights for our stay so couldn't try that. Lucky we had lots food in the van. Plus had to move our van 3 times to get a 4 night stay.
Love all the animals and rustic features, outdoor showers.
Friendly staff and camp hosts and loved the bicycle assistance to our van site.
Just felt we missed out on the atmosphere of the station and once we had done the walks a bit lost for our 4 day stay. Had a drink at the rustic bar and enjoyed the yummy scones one morning.
Went into Coral Bay for 2 different days.
Next time will have to book way in advance and try to get a Thurs to Saturday night stay.

I love Bullara Station Stay, I altered my return journey home so that I could come back and spend an extra couple of nights! The staff are so welcoming, helpful and supportive and the facilities are authentically different and beautiful. The outdoor showers are a must. The layout makes for quite a social campsite but still holds a level of privacy too. Camp kitchen has the basics but can be a little busy at peak times as there is only one cooker/oven, a kettle, toaster and BBQ grills.

Cannot thank all the Bullara family enough !
We spent 7 nights out there for the strategic position between Exmouth coast and coral bay.
The station is a wonder of nature !
So many beautiful walks . The gulf access with 4WD is simply amazing for fishing .
The Table is a unic occasion to meet new expiring people and sharing the 5 stars food is something else . Genuinely cooked and old recipes revisited !
Thanks a lot , I’ll come back for sure

Definitely a station stay that is not to be missed. The calibre of the staff is a credit - every person we have encountered has been happy, helpful and friendly. In such a diverse group of people and with customers of varying needs it’s a challenge to get this right yet Bullara has done it! Special shoutout to Matilda who has gone above and beyond for us but it’s the whole team that has made our experience feel like one big holiday within a holiday! We thi o it’s a privilege to be able to visit these operational stations and we hope the travelling community continues to respect and support the owners. The Table is great for a truly unique meal experience, the pies, sausage rolls and scones are fabulous. They now have a reverse osmosis water system providing potable water.

If you can’t get a booking on the. Ningaloo this is a great place to base yourself and do day trips but irrespective it’s a must do to stop on here for at least a couple of nights.

I couldn’t have loved this place more. You know it’s a happy place when all the people that work there are happy. Goats and cows roam around freely amongst the beautiful setting. Whoever designed this place has a very good eye. Amazing pizzas, amazing scones this morning despite the shocked faces when a Brit asked for jam on top of the cream! thank you to everyone I met and who made me feel so welcome.

Great 'village' atmosphere. Facilities are excellent, scones appropriately raved about, staff friendly and helpful and what's more the sites are spacious. They have installed a reverse osmosis water supply so potable water is now on tap. The addition of potable water means Bullara is now a much less expensive option than staying in Exmouth and is reasonably central to most of the areas attractions. No hesitation in recommending them. Oh and the sunset walk is well worth it as is the Station meat - very tasty.
Rustic outback experience 🤩

What an AMAZING Place, we had 3 Days here, showers are amazing! Not only insta worthy but actually gas system HOT! along with being fresh clean water something you don't see or get at many farm stays.
The Coffee is good (cups could be improved a bit small for the price though!) scones and pies are to die for! SOO GOOOOD!
Highly recommend you book your seat at ‘The Table’ lamb night was amazing! The chef was great.
Pizza night and live music something else you don't see on a farm station.

This place has it all, Walks, Sunsets, COWS! and Family friendly atmosphere what else can you ask for!

Thank you Bullara family we will be back again for sure!

One of the best places I've ever stayed - it's brilliant! A lovely community feel with friendly staff and lots to do on site and nearby. The food at the restaurant is incredible - 10/10 😋 Would hugely recommend.

As far as station stays go, Bullara sets the benchmark.  Close to Exmouth and Coral Bay, it's perfectly located to some of WA's most scenic coast line not to mention amazing fishing, snorkelling, whale watching and swimming with whale sharks. There are accommodation options at Bullara cater for every need, but it's the restaurant that really blows you away.  The food and ambience in the old woodshed are 5 star. A not to be missed experience.  Bullara is a must stay destination on any road trip to the north west.  Perfection Tim and Edwina well done.

Such an amazing experience. We loved staying at Bullara Station. The atmosphere is wonderful. Fantastic staff, large sites, great food, clean amenities and lots to do for kids. Makes sure you grab a scone at the cafe, and dinner and a drink at the pub. We will definitely be back.

Had 2 days unpowered here and it was fantastic. Great social camp with a theme nightly. We were here for Thursday Pizza night and the Friday burgers and I believe they did over 400 burgers with live music too. Amazing station stay.

Great atmosphere
Lovely lunch and scones

Wow, what a great place to stay and relax for a while. We wish we could have spent longer to enjoy all that was on offer. The staff were really amazing and helpful. The food was absolutely delicious. We purchased some meat from reception and it was so tasty. Would definitely stay again.
Straight to the point, don’t drive past 👌👌

What a fabulous place, whoever formulated the business model is a genius.  You feel like you’re staying on the family farm for the weekend, kids running everywhere, cows and horses roaming through the bar, people eating drinking, talking and socialising.  A wonderful experience that is reasonably priced.  Even the drinks at the bar are reasonably priced which is unusual for this part of WA.  If you are within 500km of this stay then make the detour, you won’t be disappointed. The caravan parks should watch carefully and take notes, this is exactly what people are looking for.  A big shout out to the staff and ‘camp hosts’ who clearly loved their jobs, a great effort to keep you informed and offer any assistance.  Can’t recommend this stay enough!!

It was so cosy there! With a very cute farm theme including bar and cafe, fairy lights and option for outdoor showers with sky view. Thanks for a good time! :)) The bathrooms were very nice and clean.

PS please expand the kitchen facilities available. There were so many people and only 3 hot stoves available and 2 sinks. A lot of queue :)

We have absolutely loved our stay here so much that we extended it! All the staff have been so accomodating and friendly. We booked over school holidays and Sue in reception reshuffled bookings to fit us. There are plenty of walk trails in the area so it's excellent for kids. The camp care taker is so lovely and is excellent with the kids when feeding the animals in the PM. I cannot recommend this place enough!

Thank you to the lady I met at Mukinbuden who told me about this place.  It was all, and more, as she said.  Well managed, laid out with interesting stuff everywhere.  The food was also as advertised.   One night was better than no night, 😃

What a ripper place. Managed to get the over flow area for 2 nights and one of them was burger night, worth having. We came for another night a week later on our way to Karratha. Great atmosphere and the team who seem to enjoy each other's company and willing to have a chat with patrons. Cheers Peter and Zane.

Absolutely loved my stay here!
The staff there were lovely and accommodating.
Went the extra mile to help you out.

Their burger night is a must!!!!!
Great music and atmosphere.

Had some lovely walks and things to see around the camp site with roaming sheep, horses and cows.

Definitely reccomend
Excellent park, burger night is a stand out and good access to the bottom of Exmouth gulf
Wow... Wish we'd stayed here earlier. Such a great feel to it, staff are friendly and the whole site is delightfully quirky. Will be back!

We had a great stay at Bullara in our camper. Just one night but we could have stayed one more. Friendly staff and loved the host cycling us to our pitch. We did two of the walks which were enjoyable, although we didn't see anything exciting except flies! The toilet/showers were really cool and we welcomed decent facilities. Had a great dinner at The Table and the scones were as yummy as they claimed!
Great concept,  well maintained and managed. Very friendly and welcoming staff.

Just spent an amazing week at Bullara Station Stay in our caravan. The staff are very helpful and friendly. It is such a welcoming place with fabulous food from The Table to the pies, scones and Friday night burgers. Lovely waking tracks and access to Exmouth Gulf. Highly recommend and will visit again.
