Also I forgot to mention our two small dogs had a fabulous time on the walking trails and smelling all the wildlife and farm animals.
The camp host meets you after check-in, gives you a run down on the place and escorts you to your site, asks if you have any questions.

Bullara Station is a must stay!!
It is authentic with a great atmosphere
Staff are very friendly
Amenities are good.
Really enjoyed the meals - best meat balls we have ever eaten.
Great coffee - and definitely try their sausage rolls and pies!!
Location is great too- went visited after rain and had no problem with a 4WD and caravan!! Well done guys!!
Bullara Station is an absolute gem. We stayed one night we're lucky enough to see a show while there. OMG the cafe is so so good  . We would go back anytime.
It’s a must to visit Bullara if you are in the area. If you can’t stay overnight, then just visit the cafe and have a walk around and see the quirky facilities.

Absolutely loved our Stay at Bullara Station so peaceful gorgeous country and Station
We loved it that much we returned a second time after couple days away 💜
Entertainment and Food was outstanding
Thankyou to all Staff you were super friendly and welcoming

After staying in multiple Station stays, Bullara is definitely tops in all regards!
The various types of ablutions are awesome.
The sausage rolls, pies and dinner nights were fantastic.
All of the staff were super helpful and friendly.
Other similar businesses could learn greatly from this extremely well oiled machine.
An absolute highlight of our WA journey.

We are so glad we got to experience 2 nights at Bullara. It felt like a special part of our country. Thoroughly enjoyed every aspect including the shearer’s quarters, friendly staff and amazing meals. Thanks so much for the great hospitality.
Stayed a night on our way to Exmouth. Really quirky place with lots to see and read. The cafe serves great food. Happy hour around the fire was very enjoyable.
Excellent breakfast and food available. The staff were great at what they did.
Absolutely beautiful spot here

Stayed 1 night had entertainment and a few drinks at the bar , staff and facilities were great only negative was we couldn't stay another night as they were fully booked. Will be back for another visit.
Our favourite camp stop! The bar & cafe are beautiful. The long table dinner & burger nights are amazing. Staff are wonderful. Highly recommend!
Awesome place! Great scones, burgers and pies. Good caravan sites 👌

We had 2 stays - 1 night then 3.  What a fantastic setup.  There's absolutely nothing to change.  Sites, both powered & unpowered, were just great. We struck it for fabulous weather which helped.  From booking, check-in, drinks/dinner on every occasion was truly superb. We had all meals while there and each iteration was beautifully executed.  ALL staff team members were so beautiful.  Happiness exudes from each and every one of those we met.  Tim & Edwina and their team have created quite the experience and we will be recommending to any and all who travel this way. Thanks to everyone for such an enjoyable sojourn.
Fantastic reception upon arrival by friendly staff. Well set out camping areas. Awesome showers.

It's definitely a stop over. Friday night hamburgers are a delight with both atmosphere and conversations. Our week camp in a caravan came with all the glamping facilities of a cafe, restaurant, bar, and location to the gulf and nearby Coral Bay. This place has a sense of humour that you feel during your entire stay.
Ensure you book your meal nights before arrival and enjoy.

Amazing place and campground. Range station with cows and sheeps, so beautiful details, resteraunt with amazing food and coffee, night live music and so friendly and welcoming people. Highlights are the outdoor showers, an experience I never had, so cool!

We just stayed a night, but booked the second one immediatley afterwards!
Just a lovely place.

We travelled from Coral Bay.  To have an amazing meal at the table.
Best meal in 12 months eating out.  Amazing atmosphere.  Congratulations, Toby and Maree.
And all the friendly staff.
Fantastic experience at this station stay - they have everything! Make sure you time it to enjoy their burger night on a Friday.
Nothing special if you are not planning on going to the restaurant. Other campground visitors were extremely loud. Take away from the restaurant would be a good idea.

Bullara station was a great place to stay. Really friendly and helpful hosts, quirky fun decor, clean amenities and a great bar, cafe and food. This place shows how camping can be done differently and well.
Tasty sausage rolls at the cafe. Best I have had in a while.
Everyone should spend at least one night here

What an excellent Station Stay! The owners are consistently proactive in finding ways to enhance their client's experience, making it a comfortable and enjoyable stay. It's evident that they highly value and respect their staff, as every single staff member we interacted with was happy, welcoming, and eager to assist. We stayed on the unpowered side for a day, which was our first time at a station stay, and we thoroughly enjoyed the location and delicious food. We particularly appreciated the unique approach to setup, which reminded us of a cafe, dining room, shower, and toilet, all made from old scrap items. It was truly impressive!

Wow! What a place to stay! Such a vibe! Friendly staff, spacious sites, iconic experiences. Special shoutout to our lovely camp host I think her name was Casey? Filled with info, skill and a friendly smile. We will be back. Zane was good too

Outstanding scones with jam and cream.  Great coffee and pies/sausage rolls!
Such a pleasure to find on the road!
호주 최고의 장소

We stayed on a powered slightly, sadly only for one night. One of the highlights of our west coast trip. The station is phenomenal. A stunning campsite, with amazing facilities and lovely, very hospitable staff. The coffee, pies and scones were amazing. Could not recommend highly enough.
Great spot, great people, we will be back for sure👍

Sehr schöner Ort für einen Zwischenstopp!
Wir waren am Freitag zur Burgernight dort.
Traumhafte Kulisse und schmackhafte Burger.
Livemusik.
So schön ist Australien und seine Bewohner.

Vielen Dank!!

🥩 🍺

We stayed here for 4 nights in Hale hut. The staff here are amazing. Friendly and helpful. We even had a call halfway through our stay to see if we needed anything. Burger night was a highlight of our trip. Great food and entertainment.

Absolutely loved our station stay, what an incredible bunch of people and such a fantastic place to stop for a few days, the food ( sausage rolls, pies, home reared porterhouse steaks and burger night), staff, appearance, entertainment couldn't of been any better thank you for a perfect stay 👍

We stayed for 4 nights at Bullara station as a halfway stop between Coral Bay and Exmouth and absolutely loved it ! You cannot go past the wonderful customer service , the cleanliness , and the way everything had been thought of to ensure your stay was wonderful. Friday night Burger and entertainment night is a must as is the delicious scones with jam and cream.A big mention to Walt who was great at making us feel very welcome when we arrived.  Thankyou to everyone at Bullara for an amazing stay ! We shall be back !

Not a bad place to stay. We camped on one of the outer powered sites which was fine but there are no garbage bins so you need to go hunting to find them. At night there are no lights  so will also need a torch to get around. Scones,Sausage Roll and coffee were nice. Burger Night kicks off at 6pm on Friday however we weren't fed until around 7.30 but still enjoyed the burger. Showers and toilets are clean. Wildlife and farms animals wonder through the Station which was pretty cool. Couple of power blackouts thoughout the stay so switched the fridge to gas otherwise would have lost our contents.

A Pilbara resident of 13 years and this was our first visit. We will be back! Friday night Burgers and fun at The Woolshed was great! BYO drinks and chairs. Gav the musician was fantastic! The whole outfit is well organised with laid back friendly staff. The bathroom and camp kitchen amenities are unique in a modernised outback fashion, kept clean and well stocked. Highly recommend stopping here! We will be back for a dining experience at The Table next time (Sun-Wed) The menu looked awesome!
Fantastic experience! Staff super friendly, facility very clean, entertainment and food next level! 🫶

Stayed in one of the Safari Huts for a couple of nights, using Bullara as a base to visit both Coral Bay & Exmouth. The hut was amazing, clean and comfortable for 4 of us and in a reasonably quiet area of the station. We ate both nights at the station and the food and service are absolutely first class - well thought out original meals that were perfectly cooked & presented. Staff were all lovely and very helpful, breakfast scones (cream teas) are delicious. Thanks, would definitely stay again.
Loved every minute of our stay here (2 nights in the Hale Hut).

Not my first visit and won't be the last.

A last minute booking, literally on the day, meant not many accommodation options available  but hubby and I were happy to book two single dongas ("outcamp") each with air con, shower,toilet and a king single bed.

Comfy and clean so no complaints.

Friday night is burger night with live entertainment which was so delish and I think everyone in camp attended.

The cafe's breakfast options was sooo good.

This time of year bring your fly nets, their friendly.

Thanks to the staff, they're all really amazing.

What a great Station Stay!! This is one of our favourite places to stay in Australia.. With such proactive owners always striving to make things better, and more comfortable for their customers.  It is obvious they value and appreciate their staff.. Every, and I mean every staff member was happy, welcoming, and super friendly... The food is to die for 🤤🤤 AMAZING!! We stayed here and travelled down to Coral Bay for day trips.. would not even consider staying at Coral Bay while Bullara Station is just up the road.. Thank you 🙏🏻 and we will surely be back again to visit .

On the way to Exmouth. Amazing campground, best one we visited. Thought about all the details. Beautiful showers. Burger night was fantastic. Onsite cafe with barista coffee and treats was amazing. Highly recommend!

We stayed at Bullara for two nights in our van before and after travelling up to Exmouth and Cape Range as we loved the first night so much!
The atmosphere and facilities are fantastic and the staff were all brilliant and incredibly friendly.
The first night there was great live music in the bar!
The second night we booked for the Tuesday table dinner. This was a Bullara sirloin roast with veg and polenta and a basque cheesecake for dessert. The whole meal was spectacular but the cheesecake might be the best dessert I’ve ever had!
Would highly recommend staying the night or grabbing a bite if you’re passing!
PS the scones aren’t bad either!
We thoroughly enjoyed our stay. Staff were extremely professional and friendly. We loved the rustic outback feel, amenities, bar and dining areas. Great walks.
What an absolute gem of a place. The staff are fantastic and friendly.
Le cadre de ce camping est vraiment top, la décoration des espaces communs est particulièrement impressionnante ! Et le staff super sympa 🤩

We stayed here for their first night of the season, such a lovely surprise. The atmosphere is simply amazing and charming, the decoration is worth mentioning, it’s really well done. We couldn’t do the table dinner because it was booked out but it looked so fun, please book it if you can!
The camping is in the middle of nowhere which can be weird but it’s definitely worth the stop!
The cafe is the morning with the scones and carrot cake was delicious and so relaxing.
Last but not least: the staff is so welcoming and friendly, 5 stars only!!

Ps: bring fly nets!
So much fun, great vibe without ever being tacky. We stayed 3 nights and had a blast

During my recent trip across W.A., I stopped by Bullara Station Stay. The cafe caught my eye with its beautiful rustic charm, offering both indoor and outdoor dining options. While I didn't stay overnight, the cafe and restroom facilities were a welcome pitstop. The station's ambiance adds a unique touch to the journey through Western Australia.

Was soll man da sagen, diese Farm mit Campingplatz ist einfach nur cool...gepflegt..jedes Detail ein echtes Highlight...Dusche🥰🥰🥰...man kann auch Fleisch aus der eigenen Zucht kaufen, die Bar /Restaurant...toll gemacht...ich konnte mich kaum satt sehen an den vielen schönen Sachen

What comes to my mind when I think about Bullara station is : fabulous !
We went there on the recommendation of friends and were not disappointed. Far from this ! We were extremely lucky to be there for the last burger night of the season, which was incredible. And also John's damper… we got a real treat for our taste buds hey ! The atmosphere « made in Bullara »  is unique. The staff are exceptionally friendly, making sure you feel at home from the moment you arrive. The place, its layout, decoration etc are just magnificent. In short, you've got it : go and book your night with them asap ! To tell you the truth, we came all the way from France to experience it ! And we even extended our stay because we felt so good there. 11 out of 10 for us ! Again : a huge thank you for making our stay wonderful. Xx
Closed for summer unfortunately... Opening april 1st 2024 according to their little road sign.

We stayed two nights with our motorhome and enjoyed it very much. Our only wish would be a nicer playground with a sun cover and we had the feeling the park is not so kids friendly. We saw a lot of kids but they did not know what to do in the park. Most of them
were sitting in the Woolshet watching iPad.

We hired one the houses on the station (hartlys).
Loved it.
Station is ideally located for visiting Coral Bay, Exmouth etc.
We ate dinner at their restaurant on 3 occasions, the food was excellent.
They also have a small Cafe open in the mornings for barrista coffee,  cakes sausage rolls, pies etc.
Loved our stay there.

A beautiful campsite!
We stayed at Bullara two nights and had the best time. We were not expecting finding something like this. It feels like being in a western movie. It creates a friendly vibe, the staff is the most welcoming, they really made us feel at home so thank you to all of them.
The restaurant is amazing! The concept is to sit around a huge table with plenty other people and eat the most delicious meal.
We got to share a wonderful dinner with wonderful people.
We really recommend staying at Bullard Station, there is so much to do and it is located between Coral Bay and Exmouth.
If we had more time we would have stayed a week 😁

It’s hot it’s dusty, it’s Friday afternoon and the place is filling up quickly.
As you turn off the tar onto the  1 plus kilometres of dirt drive way to Bullard Station, you know you are in for something different.
For a start it’s a working station, with a camp ground attached, on the way to the camp ground you are introduced to some art, some history and some quirky stuff.
Once register at the office you wait for your greeter on a push bike, to give you a little overview of the camp and show you to your site.
Sites are spread around, some in the cattle yard, others over yonder and some down the back, but no matter where you are, you are a short walk to the main area.
Drones are not permitted in the camp area, as some of the showers don’t have roofs, they are open air, 4 walls and a watering can hanging from above.
Time for a wander round, looking at all the quirky things on show, to late for scones they finished at 1:00pm.
Back to camp freshen up, sit back and relax as the clock ticks towards burger and live entertainment time.
Great time to chat to the other campers, see where they have been and where they are going.
The kids well their up with the animals.
Just after 5 there is movement at the station, as the carpark has been closed & the camp chairs are on the move, some 350 of them actually, all individually carried by the guests as they bag their spot for the entertainment and burger cook up.
At six our host hops up on stage to explain what will happen over the next hour, as the staff deliver 350 delicious burgers in a fashion that fast food joints have long forgotten.
45 minutes later everybody is feed & what a burger it was.
In short Bullara Station is not just a camp, it’s a destination. A place to come to where the staff want you to enjoy, where old and broken become art and quirky. A place to enjoy & perhaps even take a bit of fresh meat away with you.
Scones are on from 8 in the morning and yes they are just as good as the burgers.

We came just for the scones (delightful) and were pleasantly surprised by the friendly horses and sheep. Spent a good hour or two just kicking back and soaking up the great atmosphere. Whoever is behind the designing is very talented!

We absolutely loved our 7 night stay here. We had a powered site with plenty of room. Friendliest staff, including the horses 🐎.  Excellent food and station events. Indifferent sheep 🐑 that seem to judge you 😆. The 4WD to the Gulf is fantastic, especially when the tide is racing in. Great position to explore both Exmouth and Coral Bay. Loved our bush shower...divine ❤️
Amazing, one-of-a-kind Station Stay. Great service and very unique experience given the restaurant, bar and cafe!
Awesome camping spot - great set up, friendly staff, great coffee (waking up at a campsite and being able to get a good coffee was lovely). Loved the showers and being able to meet the animals.

We have stayed at a number of farm stays in our caravan but this one takes the cake. Brilliantly conceived and executed, it was a pleasure to spend four nights here. Great setting, quirkily set up, free entertainment and the best food.

Bullara station is our favourite outback station stay in all of Australia  With wonderful grounds, animals, burger night, live music, damper and fireside chats, a delicious cafe, tracks to the gulf, and such a relaxed atmosphere. You could easily spend weeks unwinding here. The staff are so friendly and the owners are always out saying hello. Theres so much to do but also nothing that needs to be done. It is a must visit location when travelling Australia. Thankyou Bullara we will be back again one day.
Loved our stay

Bullara station was amazing! The staff were very friendly upon arrival, the staff on bikes were amazing and I think it’s a great idea how they take you to site. I can go on and on about how great this station was! Highly recommend, you can’t miss this stay. The scones were the best nice and big. Everything from walks, burger night, showers, animals, games and house keeping is all just perfect.

Thankyou
Australian culture at its best with every stay memorable! Love this place!

We recently stayed here for one night. Our campsite was in a cattle yard which was unique!

Facilities were spotless and really funky.

We enjoyed dinner in the woolshed and were extremely impressed at the food that the chef produced.

Booking, check in and out were efficient and easy.

We stayed the first & last week of June & loved it
burger night 450 burgers in an hour & a half was fun to see, the damper making & tasting a must, we ate at the restaurant on one occasion on lamb night which was faultless, very difficult to get the local beef at the time we were there due to popularity so enquire when you check in & queue up early, the drive to the Gulf was interesting
Well worth a week stay, we didn’t have a time limit hence staying the 2 weeks

Paid a fortune to wash my car when leaving Exmouth. Shortly after arrival at Bullara Station I was parked next to the camp kitchen. The water truck driver found it funny to completely spray my car with bore water. Now it is covered with baked on white spots which are hard to remove. Not happy.

Stayed for the burger night. Entertainment was not appropriate for the crowd. Solo singer that put a twist on every song. Most people got up after their burger and went back to their caravan. The dynamics of the crowd was grey nomads and young families. Staff amazing. Very friendly and hard working. Pir stay was great, but probably 2 to 3 nights is long enough.

Such a funky farm! We stayed 2 nights here and was fantastic. Being off the land myself, i didn't think the "farm stay" would be hardly as new for me as it would be for others, however it felt like a whole new world. The showers & toilets were very clean and sooooo fun with all of the old tools etc. Everything was in good proximity to each other and we had a very large site. The resident sheep were friendly also. I couldn't rate this place higher and we'll definitely be back if ever over in WA. 👏
Clean amnenities staff very helpful lots of things to do.  Buger night was outstanding

Great place for good coffee.
Great beef in the reception fridge to purchase.
One of the must do station stays, working farm with excellent old wool shed renovated to cafe and relaxing meeting place. Some shade in a dusty environment, bring your drinking water.
Staff could be a little more friendly
Couldn't cram more people in if you tried. And can someone please shut that low humming up at 8pm?
Always a pleasure to return. Always improving. Sausage rolls the best! Till next year.

Hands down the best caravan park we have stayed in. We’re not usually ones for CPs but this would be one I’d happily return to. We had an unpowered site for our stay, next to the Beer Bottle walk, which was convenient for us. We made use of all their walking tracks. We had the scones for breakfast one morning, which were delicious! Unfortunately, we didn’t realise the breakfast wraps were only served on weekends🤦‍♀️ Had we realised, we would have switched our breakfasts around. The sausage rolls were soooo good, as was the carrot cake. We also tried Damper John’s damper. We made use of the oven in the camp kitchen and baked a loaf of bread ourselves.

We booked in for Sunday night dinner and had the fish, which was so deliciously tasty. The wait staff and kitchen staff do such an amazing job 👍 my only feedback is that it is so noisy, you almost have to shout to the person over the table from you. Other than that, we thoroughly enjoyed our stay and will likely be back when in the area again.

On the day we left there was a problem with their meat fridge which meant the reception staff weren’t sure where the meat had been moved to, so we were unable to buy the meat we wanted. Thumbs up to management and staff for making this a great place to stay.

We stayed at Bullara Station for 3 nights in an unpowered caravan site. This place is fantastic. Each site has plenty of room, water is available and is so quiet even though it was fairly busy. Great location for seeing Coral Bay and Exmouth, plus the Cape Range National Park as it is around half way between both. The set up there is excellent with walks, cafe (delicious scones) & overall theme that is typical of a working station. They even sell their own meat that is so tender. Staff were excellent and would highly recommend Bullara Station if you are in the area. Beats a caravan park any day.

We stayed two nights, then came back just for the scones. Best station stay on the area by a mile. Obviously very well loved, the owners take pride in making this place exceptional. Both the tent sites and the sheerers' quarters were top rate. Book in advance or you'll miss out!
Fantastic stay. Will recommend this stay. Great amenities and wonderful staff, especially the park managers. Well done. Didnt stay in rooms, but sites were very generous.

We enjoyed our stay at Bullara. Great hot quirky showers & bathrooms, also artwork around the park. Good scones at Cafe. They have dinners that you can book ahead & we had yummy pizza which van comes in on Thursday nights. We will be back!
A great stay with great food and coffee
Had a great camp spot with shade and close to the lava trees

Wow, what a place. This place is off the chart great. Scones are just next level. Sausage rolls are hands down the best anywhere in the country. The scenery is just amazing. The facilities are quirky and rustic, but that all adds to its charm. Staff are the most welcoming and friendly people I think I have ever met. We still have burger night to come, and if what we have experienced so far is anything to go on, it will be a knock out. If I could rate this place out of 10 it would be a 15. We have already booked for next year and we will be staying longer.

**Update**
Burger night has to be experienced to be believed. Just do it and experience the best burgers on the planet, hands down. Absolutely a fantastic night. Thank you Tim, Ed, and all the staff for the most amazing time.

Bullara Station is an exceptional place to visit. A perfect mix of station life, outback and hospitality. The happiest staff that we've seen in all of Australia lead by the owners Tim and Edwina. Plan this stay and book early so that you don't miss out!
Alternative stopover to Coral Bay/Exmouth although not really practical for the reef. Bell glamping tents very nice

Spent 5 nights at bullara great spot to base yourself to explore the surrounding area. Acess to the gulf via the station track is an added bonus even caught a few fish in the tidal creek. The burger night and live entertainment was a highlight of our stay, friendly staff great food and novelty showers. Plenty of walking tracks close to the station and fire pits to borrow for the essential nightly camp fire. A unique place great for families well done bullara

This was our second visit in our caravan wonderful food for dinner at the Woodshed a great opportunity to meet other travellers . The scones are to die for and the quirky ablutions worth seeing highly recommend.

Stayed in Hale Hut. Too marks to reception staff and to chef Toby and team for a brilliant seafood dinner, a beautiful meal and great service staff. Plus wonderful adjustments for one of our party with specific dietary needs.
Hale Hut was comfortable and well equipped, great barbecue, but second bedroom has no external windows and the third bedroom is accessed from the verandah. Toilet and bathroom noise also quite bad for these bedrooms. So we had some reservations about the accommodation.
Suggestion to staff - we arrived early and would have appreciated Station map, which we requested but did not get until we formally checked in.  Provide this on arrival if people are early, so they can explore.
Note: Worth the trip out on the 4WD track to the gulf.

Such an amazing place. We just spent 3 wonderful nights in a bell tent. The tent was beautiful and the bed so comfy. It was tucked away from the rest of the campsite with its own BBQ kitchen area and the best bit, an outside shower. A hot moonlight shower is the best. The food was wonderful and all the staff so happy and helpful. I was so relaxed by the end I felt like I was floating. Thanks Bullara Station, I can’t wait to come back.

Fantastic place to stay.
Staff are very very friendly and helpful 👍

Bullara station is an incredible spot to stay, its a hidden gem in outback Western Australia.
Seemingly out the way but well worth the stay.
The service and incredible hospitality from. The owners, the views, the station itself and rugged landscape is stunning.
The food, coffee and meals at their woodshed diner exceeded expectations by a country mile!
What a treasure.
The accommodation slides in scale from tents, outback rooms and cottages but it's easy to lean into the outdoorsy feel here and soak in the stars, red sand hills, sunsets and all the animals on the property!!.

I didn’t know what to expect when staying here as we usually pick places closer to towns and/or the ocean.

We were so surprised with everything they had to offer, from the cool campsites in old cattle yards to the evening entertainment they planned around the campfire at the camp kitchen.
Unfortunately we weren’t there for their famous burger night but we got to have their famous scones for breakfast along with some really good coffees. I also tried their home made sausage roll which, in my opinion, is to die for and should be as famous as everything else here.
The drive to the mangroves and mud flats are worth a look, just watch out for the local crocodile!!

The camp hosts were all super friendly and everything was organised really well.
We will definitely be stopping here again on our way through to Exmouth.

Thanks Bullara what a fantastic place so we organised. Camp host Dean just a great guy showed to our site told us all about this great station. The other host were also awesome all the volunteers great service. Love ya
Lovely and clean facilities. Great atmosphere. Close to main highways.
It was so nice to camp here!! Highly recommended
Wonderful experience. Scones and Burger night were amazing. Great vibe

We enjoyed our stay everytime we are here. It’s very special place. The animal, the walking trails, the quirk decor around the place, the staffs and oh the food at the woodshed. We love the dinner at the woolshed, always very yummy.

What an AWSOME place!! No wonder everyone talks about it!! We checked in today, had pizza outside the Woolshed in the shade of the trees on a comfy lounge!! Will go over in the morning for coffee & scones yummy! Camp fires all around the park, happy friendly people. Love it here!!
Didn't stay but notice they cafe it was lovely.
We stayed five nights here and loved it. There is so much to do without even leaving the property. The staff are friendly and the food is home made and incredibly delicious.
A must do experience! The outdoor showers and toilet are very quirky.  The food at the dinner was amazing - very fresh.and local. Lovely staff
Excellent place to pull up for a few nights and  either chill with a coffee/tea and really nice scones but also use it as a base to visit the Exmouth Gulf area.
Excellent station stay. Organized, well equipped to cater for visitors with lots of nice camping touches. Highly recommend

Showers were amazing! Facilities are so clean, such a breathe of fresh air, never felt cleaner camping!
The scones provided in the cafe are so delicious. Definitely worth staying at for even a night if you are in Exmouth.

Absolutely loved it, staff are friendly, camping site is wonderfully done incorporating old water tanks as showers etc but everything is immaculately clean. An upgraded camp kitchen would make it a perfect 10

Everything is awesome. Absolutely recommend to stay here. We booked without knowing where we were going and we found this spectacular theme park. Staff is friendly, amenities look simple but they are unique, the vibe is great.

What a great place to have a break!  Very spacious and clean rooms.  The outdoor showers are very cool and an amazing experience.  When you go make sure you do one of the dinner nights - the food was incredible - local, fresh and so well cooked.

Fantastic experience
I highly recommend booking in for the tables dinner on Saturday night, food was sensational.
Great quiet location to stay, there are accommodation options other than caravan sites to choose if want .

We are staying in the shearer’s quarters. They are delux as far as shearer’s quarters are concerned!  The whole place has been upgraded but has totally kept the original charm.
Live comedy on tonight (from Melbourne comedy festival), pizza is also on Thursday nights.
Camp hosts are fantastic and very helpful.
We think we are in for a great night with loads of laughs in beautiful surroundings.
It may not have the beaches of coral bay and Exmouth but you should not drive past this place with at least stopping for some scones and cream!
Totally deserves 5 stars!
Love this place. Great sites. Good size. Host's and staff amazing. Beautiful part of the world
Camped at this working cattle station in the middle of nowhere & found a wonderful oasis, rustic charm & a real feel for the outback. Hot showers, cafe, live music & wonderful evening meals.

Stunning features, good food and coffee.
Nice walk trails. Large flat caravan sites.
Lots on offer for all edges.

Great place to stay.  This station is uniquely Australian and has quirky memorabilia, outdoor showers, damper and burger nights, and totally set up for families.  Bore water was very drinkable when we were there. Great coffee and scones.
Heard the scones were sensational and they weren’t wrong! Such a great little coffee stop - thank you!

A very popular site, located on an active cattle station in reach of Coral Bay and Exmouth. The toilets flush and the showers have plenty of hot water if a little rustic with the water plunging from holes in rusty buckets and a little difficult to regulate. The kitchen is inadequate for such a large site with many people crowded round an old four burner standard kitchen hob. The cafe serves good coffee and other typical offerings. This place is now a big commercial business doing very well, probably better to have visited it before the business and  popularity took off and some of the charm dissipated. Expensive for what it is and a one off.. All staff were very friendly however.

Absolut einzigartiger Ort! Bei der Ankunft wurde man von einer Person auf dem Fahrrad zu seinem Platz begleitet. Es gibt viele kurze Wanderstrecken im Umkreis. Der Campingplatz ist etwas abseits gelegenen und es gibt einige Tiere (Kühe, Schafe ect.). Die Atmosphäre ist sehr schön und familiär, obwohl es doch ein recht großer Platz ist. Abends wurden Damper gemacht und alle haben sich vor der Campkitchen vor dem Feuer versammelt. Ein guter Stopp auf dem Weg Richtung Exmouth.

An amazing y in every way. Great camp-fires dotted all around. Open air showers. Bore water is surprisingly good,  but not drinkable. Cafe and restaurant are great, and the scones are delicious.  Damper is made, and meet and greet when you arrive is really good, telli g you all you need to know.

Only incredible things to say about Bullara!

We'd heard good things and were definitely not disappointed!

Starting with our camp hosts who show every single vehicle personally to their site. They've got the best personalities and gusto and you can really tell they care about their jobs.

Burger night was sensational! We were a bit worried about how they were going to feed so many people efficiently, but they absolutely nailed it! We met so many new people and the burgers were incredible!!!! Tasted so good! And the station staff serving everything up were in such great moods and high spirits.

Saturday night we went to damper night and camp host John (i think) does such an amazing job at hosting damper night. He's so passionate about including everyone and getting the history of the station out! Not to mention his damper truly was bloody incredible!

Sunday morning we had the famous scones and were definitely not disappointed! Tasted so good and they were a great size for the price too

We followed this up with a trip out to the gulf where we met some people we spent the day with

Finished it up with a walk to the sunset hill where my boyfriend proposed!

Safe to say we loved it here and will definitely be back!
